rulings.Case Title: TALITHA BALL et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 17, 2012, Line 20, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, HEATHER FONG AND ERNEST FERRANDO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS Denied as to issue 1 and granted as to issues 2-5. As to issue one, there is a triable issue whether there was advance notice to the occupants before the house was entered. As to issues 2-5, Defendants satisfied their intial burden of production as to issues and plaintiff did not present evidence creating any triable issues. Plaintiff's request for a continuance does not comply with the requirements of Code of Civil Procedure 437c(h). The defendants are required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: BURLINGAME INVESTMENT CORPORATION et al VS. HERMAN KWAI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 17, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT CHININ TANA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS Denied in its entirety. There are triable issues whether counsel's filings and communications were made in good faith and without malice, whether those communications disrupted plaintiff's relationship with counsel and caused damages, and whether defendant engaged in wrongdoing and/or is liable as a co-conspirator for co-defendants' alleged wrongdoing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: FRANK ALIOTO et al VS. WILLIAM F. GARLOCK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Hamilton, Ricci & Associates, Inc. Motion To Order Deeming All Hamilton Ricci'S Requests For Admissions To Plaintiff'S Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday Feb. 17, 2012 Line 4 - Defendant Hamilton, Ricci & Associates, Inc.'s Motion To Order Deeming All Hamilton Ricci'S Requests For Admissions To Plaintiff'S Admitted.Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge.The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Grant, no opposition filed. Moving party to appear with proposed order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mschreiber@cwclaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JANINE BOCCIARDI VS. APPEARANCE ENHANCEMENT CLINIC, A MEDICAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 17, 2012, Line 7, DEFENDANT FRED SUESS, M.D. DBA APPEARANCE ENHANCEMENT CLINIC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS Denied in its entirety. The motion for summary judgment is denied because the sole issue articulated in the moving parties' separate statement expressly pertains only to the first cause action and does not address the other three claims alleged by plaintiff. The motion for summary adjudicated is denied because the sole issue articulated in the moving parties' separate statement does not resolve the entirety of the first cause of action. Even surpassing these procedural deficiencies, the motion is denied because there is a triable issue whether Plaintiff gave informed consent to the surgical procedure performed by Dr. Suess. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 17, 2012, Line 7, PLAINTIFF JANINE BOCCIARDI'S Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint is Granted. In light of the order denying defendants' first summary motion and the tentative ruling denying defendants' second summary motion, because there is no prejudice to defendants by granting this motion, the strong policy favoring permitting amendments requires the granting of the motion. rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE BATLIN VS. LANGE TRUCKING INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Compliance With Subpoena For Business Records And Mtn For Sanctions And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday Feb. 17, 2012 Line 2 -DEFENDANT LANGE TRUCKING INC., RODGERS, JOHN HOWARD's Amended Motion To Compel Compliance With Subpoena For Business Records And Motion For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge.The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Deny. No proof of service onthird-party deponent per California Rule of Court 3.1346. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mschreiber@cwclaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JONATHAN HERSCHEL BORNSTEIN VS. RUFUS THAYER III et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Petition To Confirm Attorney Client Fee Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 17, 2012, Line 12, PLAINTIFF JONATHAN BORNSTEIN'S Notice Of And Petition To Confirm Attorney Client Fee Arbitration Award IS Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: KATHRYN E. NEWBURN VS. CALIFORNIA BOARD OF REGISTERED NURSING et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 17, 2012, Line 13, PETITIONER KATHRYN NEWBURN'S Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate IS Granted. rulings.Case Title: IN RE: MARCIA SANCHEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Marcia Sanchez, Alfred Randolph Petitio For Order Authorizing Pre-Filing Discovery Per Ccp 2035.010 Et Seq
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday Feb. 17, 2012 Line 5 - Petitioner Marcia Sanchez, Alfred Randolph's Petition For Order Authorizing Pre-Filing Discovery Per Ccp 2035.010. Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge.The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing.=(302/JPT)
